verdict_reasoning: IZUMO4 is a member of the IZUMO family, a group of proteins involved in reproductive biology and gamete fusion. The NCBI summary explicitly places it in the nucleus. No documented outer-leaflet display in cancer, cell death, or activation states; no evidence of stable TM-partner tethering, lysosomal exocytosis, or PM minority cycling pool. The IZUMO family comprises several single-pass TM proteins critical for sperm-oocyte fusion (IZUMO1, IZUMO2/SPAG16L, IZUMO3), but IZUMO4 has no published evidence of TM anchoring or surface display; it is nuclear-resident. No cell-surface-directed clinical programs target IZUMO4. No documented dual localization or ectodomain shedding.
predicted_key_uncertainty: IZUMO4 is a family member within a germline-reproductive protein group; family members IZUMO1/2/3 are surface-anchored sperm and oocyte fusins. The nuclear localization call for IZUMO4 is from a brief NCBI summary. Gene-specific surface-proteomics or intact-cell flow-cytometry data for IZUMO4 itself is not readily available in the public record. If IZUMO4 has a documented gamete-restricted surface isoform or trafficking pool, that would shift to `contextual` / `tissue_restricted_surface`, but current evidence supports nuclear residence.
